properties.name: Camp Sakima
properties.description: A camp on the S-F Scout Ranch operated by the Greater St. Louis Area Council, serving as a home for National Youth Leadership Training and Scouts BSA summer camp programs.

properties.name: Cow Creek Scout Reservation
properties.address: 2658 Cow Creek Rd, Blue Eye, MO 65611
properties.description: An Ozark Trails Council camp located on the shores of Table Rock Lake near Blue Eye, offering waterfront activities and scenic Ozarks camping.

properties.name: H. Roe Bartle Scout Reservation
properties.address: 5525 NE Scout Camp Rd, Osceola, MO 64776
properties.description: A 4,200-acre reservation in the Osage hills operated by the Heart of America Council, hosting over 6,000 Scouts annually across three separate camps.

properties.name: Lake of the Ozarks Scout Reservation
properties.address: 525 Camp Hohn Dr, Gravois Mills, MO 65037
properties.description: A 450-acre Great Rivers Council reservation with a mile and a half of shoreline on the Osage Arm of the Lake of the Ozarks.

properties.name: S-F Scout Ranch
properties.description: A 5,200-acre wooded property operated by the Greater St. Louis Area Council south of Farmington, encompassing multiple camps including Famous Eagle, Gamble, Sakima, and Swift High Adventure Base.

properties.name: Swift High Adventure Base
properties.description: The Greater St. Louis Area Council's high adventure base on the S-F Scout Ranch, offering Venturing summer camp programs with challenging outdoor activities.

properties.name: Bay Springs Scout Base
properties.description: A primitive tent camping facility operated by the Natchez Trace Council, situated on a peninsula surrounded by Bay Springs Lake about 30 minutes east of Booneville.

properties.name: Camp Binachi
properties.description: A historic council camp in east-central Mississippi operated by the Choctaw Area Council for over a century, featuring Lake Frank Williams and Binachi Shooting Sports.

properties.name: Camp Currier
properties.description: A 300-acre year-round camp and training facility built in 1925 by the Chickasaw Council on land donated by Elizabeth Currier, located near Arkabutla Lake south of Memphis.

properties.name: Camp Seminole
properties.description: A year-round resident camping facility operated by the Natchez Trace Council, located 10 minutes north of Starkville with tent and cabin camping, a dining hall, and shooting ranges.

properties.name: Camp Tiak
properties.description: Opened in 1955 and operated by the Pine Burr Area Council, Camp Tiak takes its name from the Choctaw word meaning 'Tall Pines,' reflecting the towering pine forests of southern Mississippi.

properties.name: Camp Toby Tubby
properties.description: A Natchez Trace Council camp on Sardis Lake about 20 minutes northwest of Oxford, featuring developed tent camping with fire rings and access to mountain bike trails.

properties.name: Camp Yocona
properties.description: The Natchez Trace Council's primary summer camp facility with a tradition dating back to 1947, located less than 40 minutes from Tupelo.

properties.name: Hood Scout Reservation (Warren Hood SR)
properties.address: 8065 Old Port Gibson Rd, Hazlehurst, MS 39083
properties.description: A 1,300-acre reservation in the rolling hills of southwestern Mississippi operated by the Andrew Jackson Council, declared one of the Top 10 Scout Camps in the country.

properties.name: Salmen Scout Reservation (Camp V-Bar)
properties.description: A 1,400-acre reservation operated by the Southeast Louisiana Council, located about 90 minutes from New Orleans with a lake, swimming pool, and lighted COPE course.

properties.name: Camp Arrowhead (Ozark Trails Council)
properties.description: The oldest Scout camp west of the Mississippi River, established in 1924 and operated by the Ozark Trails Council on 557 wooded acres near Marshfield.

properties.name: Base Camp (Northern Star Council)
properties.description: Northern Star Council's indoor activity center at historic Fort Snelling, featuring a TeamBuilding Center with rock climbing, ropes courses, and archery, plus a Leadership Center housing council headquarters.

properties.name: Camp Wilderness (Northern Lights Council)
properties.description: Northern Lights Council's premier camping and high adventure facility, a 2,400-acre outdoor classroom situated between Bad Axe and Mantrap Lakes in the heart of the Paul Bunyan State Forest.

properties.name: Cedar Point Scout Camp
properties.description: A Twin Valley Council camp near the Iowa border offering woods and waterfront activities including canoeing, kayaking, and stand-up paddleboarding.

properties.name: Cuyuna Scout Camp
properties.description: A 684-acre Twin Valley Council camp in a heavily wooded area north of Brainerd, bordering six lakes.

properties.name: Fred C. Anderson Scout Camp
properties.address: 186 Andersen Scout Camp Trail, Houlton, WI 54082
properties.description: A Northern Star Council camp located on the St. Croix River just five miles northeast of Stillwater, Minnesota, offering Scouts BSA and Venturing summer camp programs in a riverside woodland setting.

properties.name: Gamehaven Scout Camp
properties.description: Established in 1995 by the Gamehaven Council, this 190-acre camp southeast of Rochester features a developed upper camp area for Cub Scouts and a rustic camp area for older groups.

properties.name: Kiwanis Scout Reservation
properties.address: 15987 St. Croix Trail N, Marine on St. Croix, MN 55047
properties.description: A 110-acre Northern Star Council camp nestled on the banks of the St. Croix River, operating as a camp since 1923.

properties.name: Many Point Scout Camp
properties.address: 41408 Many Point Scout Camp Rd, Ponsford, MN 56575
properties.description: A 1,700-acre Northern Star Council camp on Many Point Lake with over 6 miles of shoreline, bordering the Tamarac National Wildlife Refuge.

properties.name: Norseland Scout Reservation
properties.description: A 56-acre Twin Valley Council camp on the shores of Sand Lake, approximately 12 miles northwest of St. Peter.

properties.name: Parker Scout Reservation
properties.description: A 256-acre Central Minnesota Council camp established in 1941 on North Long Lake north of Brainerd, featuring twelve campsites, a private beach, and shooting sports ranges.

properties.name: Phillippo Scout Reservation
properties.description: A 450-acre Northern Star Council camp on the south shore of Byllesby Reservoir, featuring rolling fields, wooded campsites, and hills overlooking the Cannon River valley.

properties.name: Rum River Scout Camp
properties.address: 15659 Saint Francis Blvd NW, Ramsey, MN 55303
properties.description: A 160-acre Northern Star Council camp located 4 miles north of Anoka, featuring wooded campsites, three enclosed activity buildings, and year-round programming.

properties.name: Stearns Scout Camp
properties.description: A 1,200-acre Northern Star Council camp featuring open fields, rolling hills, and thick forest, suitable for small overnight campouts or large events.
